summary: paperclip-host-utils postinstall exfiltrates host identity to hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: On `npm install`, dist/postinstall.js spawns a detached `/bin/sh` process (cwd=/, stdio ignored) that uses curl/wget to send the installer's `whoami` and `hostname` plus a timestamp to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://185.112.147.174:8080/callback over plain HTTP. The lifecycle hook runs automatically at install time without user interaction, and the detached/silent execution shape is deliberately evasive. The destination is an unauthenticated attacker-controlled IP with no relation to any legitimate package function, and the collected identifiers give the operator a per-host beacon suitable for tracking compromised installers and staging follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
